methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 24 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
DroneShield operates in the Defense & Security sector as a public company, having raised over $100M to date. The broader sector includes 24 peers, though the company's specific stage and scale yield a sparse comparable set: 2 public peers and 8 private companies identified, with top private counterparts including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries.

Peer-median valuation proved inapplicable to this analysis. The Defense & Security universe, while populated, lacks sufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables—zero datapoints met the threshold of ≥3 peers at matching stage or valuation tier. A fallback to all-sector median would introduce material bias given DroneShield's distinct market positioning relative to broader sector participants. The company's hybrid status as a public entity with specialized counter-unmanned systems places it outside standard filtering bands used in traditional venture or growth-stage analysis.

This valuation analysis is therefore constrained by data insufficiency. A credible range cannot be supported without either (1) expanding the comparable set to include adjacent Defense & Aerospace verticals, (2) applying revenue or EBITDA multiples if financial data becomes available, or (3) constructing a bottom-up discounted cash flow model using company-specific guidance. The narrow peer universe and public listing status create unique analytical challenges that standard triangulation methods do not resolve.
